---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_14.png){.title-icon} Analysis"
subtitle: "From analysis-ready dataset to results"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
**Under development.** These pages give minimal, verified examples - not a statistics textbook. They are being expanded.
:::

Once you have assembled your analysis dataset ([Phase 12](12_data-manipulation.qmd)), you are ready to analyse. This section stays **register-specific and practical**: when to use what, which packages, and a minimal example - it does not teach statistical theory. For depth, see the [Epidemiologist R Handbook](https://www.epirhandbook.com/en/), [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) and DDEA's R course (see [Learning resources](laeringsressourcer.qmd)).

::: {.callout-tip}
**Start here.** Choosing the right analysis - and making sure your data meet its **assumptions** - is the first and one of the most important steps in the analysis; choose wrong and the results won't hold, no matter how carefully the rest is done. So begin with [Choosing a statistical analysis](13a_valg-af-analyse.qmd): an overview matching data type and design to the right test/model and R command, and covering the assumptions and how to check them.
:::

## Pages in this section

Pick the pages that fit your study - not every study uses all of them.

| Page | Packages |
|---|---|
| [Descriptive tables (Table 1)](13b_deskriptive-tabeller.qmd) | `gtsummary`, `finalfit` |
| [Figures for publication](13c_figurer.qmd) | `ggplot2` |
| [Regression](13d_regression.qmd) | `glm`/`lm`, `survival::clogit` (matched case-control) |
| [Time-to-event](13e_time-to-event.qmd) | `survival`, `survminer`, `tidycmprsk` (Cox, competing risks) |

::: {.callout-note}
Remember: anything leaving DST must go through **output control** - no small cells, only aggregated results. See [Phase 14 - Export and repatriation](14_eksport-hjemsendelse.qmd).
:::
